Maritime Aquarium Welcomes New Harbor Seal In Norwalk | Norwalk, CT Patch

Skip to main content

### National Edition

- See All Communities

Thank you to our Local Business Sponsor:

###### Community Corner

# Maritime Aquarium Welcomes New Harbor Seal In Norwalk

## The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk has announced the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the facility.

###### RJ Scofield, Patch Staff

Posted Sun, Apr 26, 2026 at 12:01 pm ET

The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk announced the arrival of Kaya, a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal, on April 20, 2026. (RJ Scofield/Patch Staff)

NORWALK, CT — The Maritime Aquarium announced this week the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the Norwalk facility.

According to a news release provided by the aquarium, Kaya arrived in Norwalk earlier this month from the Aquarium of the Pacific in California and is now on exhibit at the Maritime Aquarium's Pinniped Cove exhibit.

Born on April 20, 2018, at the Aquarium of the Pacific, Kaya brings both energy and warmth to her new home, according to the Maritime Aquarium.

## Find out what's happening in Norwalkfor free with the latest updates from Patch.

Subscribe

The aquarium noted visitors can easily spot Kaya among the Norwalk facility's three other seals thanks to her uniquely spotted fur pattern.

She has also quickly adapted to her new surroundings, according to the aquarium.

## Find out what's happening in Norwalkfor free with the latest updates from Patch.

Subscribe

"This was an exciting opportunity for us not only to grow our population inside Pinniped Cove but also to collaborate with the Aquarium of the Pacific," Dylan Salamone, assistant curator at the Maritime Aquarium, said in a news release. "Kaya is doing great and adjusting well to her new habitat. She is a confident female harbor seal and took little to no time getting used to her new exhibit and counterparts. In fact, on Kaya’s first night on the exhibit, we saw her hauled out with everyone to sleep, which was a great sign."

According to the aquarium, Kaya came to the Norwalk facility through a partnership with the Association of Zoos & Aquariums and Aquarium of the Pacific as part of an ongoing effort to support animal care, welfare and conservation.

"Ambassadors like Kaya play a vital role in helping guests understand and appreciate these animals and the challenges they face," Rachel Stein, director of animal husbandry at the Maritime Aquarium, said in a news release, "and inspire them to take actions that protect harbor seals and other animals in Long Island Sound."

Guests can see Kaya in Pinniped Cove, where seal feedings take place daily at 11:30 a.m. and 3 p.m., according to the aquarium.

More information is available at maritimeaquarium.org.

# A luxurious one night visit
Review of[Great Northern Hotel, A Tribute Portfolio Hotel, London](https://www.tripadvisor.co.uk/Hotel_Review-g186338-d4053933-Reviews-Great_Northern_Hotel_A_Tribute_Portfolio_Hotel_London-London_England.html)
5.0 of 5 bubblesReviewed24 Mar 2023[via mobile](https://www.tripadvisor.co.uk/apps)
I booked this hotel for one night as I wanted to come and see a show. I decided to treat myself with this particular hotel. Thanks to my Marriott Bonvoy membership I was upgraded to an Edwardian king room on the top floor.
The room was comfy, stylish, quiet, and luxurious. I have seen many reviews say that the rooms are small. I found mine to have plenty of room for relaxing. It never felt cramped or too small. The walk in shower was lovely, and the amenities were perfect! I especially loved the mini bar and coffee machine being hidden in the wall. The wooden shutters on the window worked wonders for keeping the light out in the morning.
The staff were very friendly and helpful. I was able to get late check out and I found ordering breakfast to my room to be easy and really convenient. I also really appreciated the gentleman who showed me to my room and explained different features of the hotel.
The hotel is situated between St Pancras and Kings Cross and, as such, is fantastically located for both travel and for accessing central London.
I would definitely consider staying here again.
MoreDate of stay:March 2023
Room Tip:You’ll need your key card to get into the hotel late at night. Room service is closed between...
Trip type:Travelled solo

# History & Heritage

 

An Luxury Central London Boutique Hotel In the Heart of London’s King’s Cross St Pancras

Magnificently refurbished and designed, this iconic central London hotel brings all the elegance of its past back into the centre of London’s King’s Cross St Pancras.

When Kaya Great Northern Hotel first opened its doors in 1854, the passengers of London flocked. The hotel stood at the centre of London’s revolutionary new age of steam. Guests made the Kaya Great Northern Hotel a glamorous destination in central London and a stylish point of departure.

Now magnificently renovated, the Kaya Great Northern Hotel guarantees the glorious glamour of its iconic past is central once again to the King’s Cross St Pancras basin. Lewis Cubitt, great architect of Victorian London, crafted this hotel to catch the eye: its slender curve of robust brick still a bold stamp on a busy landscape.

From the vantage point of the Pancras Road, the Kaya Great Northern Hotel originally looked across planted gardens towards King’s Cross Station. Standing now on this plot, deliberately reciprocating Cubitt’s curve, is the new King’s Cross Western Concourse – two striking designs both central to a global transport hub.

## INSIDE THE BUILDING

Broad corridors and wrought iron staircases sweep through the hotel’s six floors. Ninety rooms lovingly restored and exquisitely re-designed – all the original grandeur of this central London hotel honoured with painstaking attention to detail. High ceilings, ceiling-to-floor windows, expansive curved corridors – all central to Cubitt’s classic design – create an overwhelming sense of light and space. With the calm and serenity of a great ocean liner, you will never believe this hotel is central to the largest transport intersection in London.

evidence.title: Kaya Great Northern Hotel Embarks on New Chapter in Partnership with HotelREZ Hotels & Resorts - HotelREZ
evidence.fetched_text:
Kaya Great Northern Hotel Embarks on New Chapter in Partnership with HotelREZ Hotels & Resorts - HotelREZ 

 

 

 

 

 

# Kaya Great Northern Hotel Embarks on New Chapter in Partnership with HotelREZ Hotels & Resorts

- October 7, 2025

Kaya Great Northern Hotel Embarks on New Chapter in Partnership with HotelREZ Hotels & Resorts https://hotelrez.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Kaya-Exterior-01.jpg 800 512

HotelREZ https://hotelrez.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Kaya-Exterior-01.jpg

London, 30 September, 2025 – Great Northern Hotel, the historic Grade II-listed hotel located in King’s Cross, London, will now operates under the Kaya Hotels & Resorts brand with a new name: Kaya Great Northern Hotel (Kaya GNH).

Now fully integrated into group’s portfolio as Kaya GNH, following its acquisition of the 88-bedroom property in June 2025, this strategic realignment underscores Kaya’s commitment to expanding its presence in London and across Europe, while placing renewed emphasis on personalised, high-quality guest experiences.

Located at one of London’s busiest transport hubs and a gateway to Europe, Kaya Great Northern Hotel combines a prime King’s Cross setting with its heritage as a Grade II-listed landmark, ensuring it remains a destination of choice for discerning travellers.

As part of this new chapter, Kaya Great Northern Hotel has also joined HotelREZ Hotels & Resorts for global distribution, sales, and marketing representation. The partnership positions the property strongly to capture both corporate and leisure markets with enhanced visibility on the GDS agency market under HotelREZ chain code HO, as well as across international booking platforms.

The hotel will be also be part of Best Loved Hotels®, a collection of unique global individual properties, the B2C consumer facing channel of HotelREZ, aimed at discerning leisure travellers and agents.

Through HotelREZ, the hotel will benefit from advanced distribution technology, including the CentralREZ CRS platform, REZmedia image content tool, REZtoMarket content services, and the Cvent Lanyon RFP platform..

Daniel Simmons, CCO at HotelREZ, said: “We are delighted to welcome Kaya Great Northern Hotel into the HotelREZ portfolio. This iconic London property has enormous potential as it repositions independently. With its strong GDS contribution and prime corporate location, we see significant opportunities to grow both corporate and leisure demand, leveraging our sales, marketing, and distribution expertise”

General Manager Kaya Great Northern Hotel, said: “As we embark on this exciting new chapter, joining HotelREZ allows us to maintain our independence while benefiting from world-class distribution and marketing. Our focus is on delivering exceptional guest experiences while strengthening our visibility across global corporate and leisure markets.”

Kaya Hotels & Resorts added: “We are delighted to have made our first international hotel investment in London. With this hotel, we aim to showcase the finest examples of Turkish hospitality and increase the number of foreign tourists visiting our hotels in Türkiye. Additionally, we are proud to be the first Turkish tourism company to make such a significant hotel investment in the UK. We believe this investment will strengthen mutual tourism relations between Türkiye and the UK. We’re excited to build on its strengths to create even more memorable experiences for our guests.”

The relaunch of Kaya Great Northern Hotel strengthens both Kaya Hotels & Resorts’ growing international footprint and HotelREZ’s London portfolio, highlighting the increasing appeal of independence for landmark properties seeking agile global representation.

Kaya Great Northern Hotel is now bookable on the GDS under HotelREZ’s HO chain code.

– ENDS –

 

 

 

Share

# History & Heritage

 

An Luxury Central London Boutique Hotel In the Heart of London’s King’s Cross St Pancras

Magnificently refurbished and designed, this iconic central London hotel brings all the elegance of its past back into the centre of London’s King’s Cross St Pancras.

When Kaya Great Northern Hotel first opened its doors in 1854, the passengers of London flocked. The hotel stood at the centre of London’s revolutionary new age of steam. Guests made the Kaya Great Northern Hotel a glamorous destination in central London and a stylish point of departure.

Now magnificently renovated, the Kaya Great Northern Hotel guarantees the glorious glamour of its iconic past is central once again to the King’s Cross St Pancras basin. Lewis Cubitt, great architect of Victorian London, crafted this hotel to catch the eye: its slender curve of robust brick still a bold stamp on a busy landscape.

From the vantage point of the Pancras Road, the Kaya Great Northern Hotel originally looked across planted gardens towards King’s Cross Station. Standing now on this plot, deliberately reciprocating Cubitt’s curve, is the new King’s Cross Western Concourse – two striking designs both central to a global transport hub.

## INSIDE THE BUILDING

Broad corridors and wrought iron staircases sweep through the hotel’s six floors. Ninety rooms lovingly restored and exquisitely re-designed – all the original grandeur of this central London hotel honoured with painstaking attention to detail. High ceilings, ceiling-to-floor windows, expansive curved corridors – all central to Cubitt’s classic design – create an overwhelming sense of light and space. With the calm and serenity of a great ocean liner, you will never believe this hotel is central to the largest transport intersection in London.

## The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk has announced the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the facility.

Posted Sun, Apr 26, 2026 at 12:01 pm ET

The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk announced the arrival of Kaya, a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal, on April 20, 2026. (RJ Scofield/Patch Staff)

NORWALK, CT — The Maritime Aquarium announced this week the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the Norwalk facility.
